title: TRAINING WORKSHOPS - 211info
text: BOARDMAN ADVOCACY CENTER TRAINING WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community training with a focus on the prevention of domestic violence, teen dating violence, elder abuse, and sexual assault as well as comprehensive training for crisis response volunteers. 200 City Center Circle Boardman, OR 97818 Eligibility: Most training classes: Unrestricted Volunteers: Age 18 and older. Must pass a background check. Hours: Tuesday 1pm-5pm by appointment only (541) 567-0424 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 567-0424 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Morrow County Services: Dating Violence Prevention Elder Abuse Prevention Sexual Assault Prevention Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Prevention Volunteer Opportunities Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CLASSES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - ADVOCACY SERVICES MUTUAL SUPPORT - SUPPORT GROUPS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. BOARDMAN ADVOCACY CENTER
